final_prompt.prompt:
Design an original, non-infringing brand color palette and typography specification sheet for 染秋 (RAN QIU), an autumn fragrance brand rooted in Eastern philosophy and traditional Chinese dyeing aesthetics.

Style/medium: brand identity style guide / color & typography spec sheet, flat editorial layout with refined Chinese design sensibility
Composition: structured spec-sheet layout — primary color swatches row at top, secondary/accent color swatches row below, typography specimens section at bottom; clean grid with labeled annotations for each swatch and typeface
Subject: brand visual identity system for 染秋 (RAN QIU) — primary colors, supporting colors, and font pairing rules; colors inspired by osmanthus gold, persimmon amber, maple crimson, oolong brown, sandalwood beige, and amber resin; typography pairing of a classical Chinese serif with a refined modern Latin counterpart
Scene/backdrop: neutral warm off-white background evoking handmade paper or washi, suggesting traditional dyeing craft
Lighting/mood: soft, warm, still — the quiet mood of an autumn afternoon; no harsh shadows
Color palette: primary — deep osmanthus gold (#C8860A range), persimmon amber-orange (#C45C26 range), aged maple crimson (#8B2E16 range); supporting — oolong dark brown (#3D2314 range), sandalwood mist beige (#E8DBC8 range), amber resin translucent gold (#D4A84B range), ink black (#1A1410 range); all tones warm, muted, autumnal
Materials/textures: subtle washi paper texture on background; ink-wash micro-texture on color swatch blocks
Text (verbatim): 染秋, RAN QIU, 主色, 辅色, 字体规范, 桂花乌龙, 琥珀枫叶, 柿霜暮色
Constraints: all color swatches must be clearly labeled with name and hex/color code annotation; typography section must show Chinese and Latin specimens side by side; layout must read as a professional brand guideline page
Avoid: overly bright or saturated Western autumn colors (pumpkin-spice orange, Halloween palette); generic flat-design style without texture; cold or blue-toned neutrals; clutter or decorative excess inconsistent with Eastern minimalism
